title: Dungeons & Desktops: Building a procedurally generated roguelike with GitHub Copilot CLI
content: The author built “GitHub Dungeons,” a terminal‑based roguelike that turns a GitHub repository into a procedurally generated dungeon using Binary Space Partitioning (BSP). Each commit seeds the layout so code changes reshape the map. Developed with GitHub Copilot CLI, the project leverages /delegate commands to offload code generation, allowing the author to focus on gameplay. Players navigate with keyboard controls, fight bugs, and try to escape over five levels. The game can be installed as a GitHub extension.
eventKey: dungeons-desktops-building-procedurally-generated-roguelike
normalizedTitle: dungeons desktops building a procedurally generated roguelike with github copilot cli

title: Lloyd Blankfein on Risk, Crisis, and Leadership
content: Lloyd Blankfein, former Goldman Sachs CEO, explains how his firm navigated the 2008 crisis by building resilience, making tough decisions, and preserving a partnership culture. He stresses risk management as both a discipline and a mindset, distinguishing between being wrong and acting recklessly. Blankfein discusses balancing risk with protection, scaling without losing culture, and the growing complexity of technology and AI, highlighting both opportunities and hazards.

title: 从会议录音到知识库全自动：我把数百段录音做成了可 RAG 问答的 Wiki（附开源代码）
content: I built an end‑to‑end pipeline that turns recorded meetings into a searchable, RAG‑ready Wiki with minimal effort. The process runs nightly and requires no manual input: audio files are split by speaker, speakers are named using a three‑stage funnel (timestamp tags, CAM++ voice matching, then LLM inference) achieving 94 % naming accuracy, enabling actionable task assignment. The pipeline then produces AI summaries and indexable Wiki pages, all powered by open‑source components (pyannote, FunASR, CAM++, OpenAI API, Python HTTP server) and available on GitHub.

title: 腾讯张军：微信已读和访客功能“已焊死”，微信状态浏览人数测试已停止
content: WeChat has stopped testing a feature that displayed how many people viewed a status, and that experiment was limited to a small iOS test. A model user clarified that statuses do not record individual viewers, and the team has ended the test. Tencent’s public‑relations director confirmed that “read receipt” and “viewer” functions are permanently disabled and will not be reintroduced. The company emphasizes that current status views are anonymous and provide only aggregate counts.

title: OpenAI 成立 OpenAI 部署公司，帮助企业构建人工智能系统
content: OpenAI has launched a deployment arm to assist businesses in building AI systems and has agreed to acquire AI consulting and engineering firm Tomoro. The new subsidiary, wholly owned by OpenAI, will offer a unified experience for clients working with either OpenAI or the deployment company. The initiative will start with an initial $4.2 billion investment to scale operations and pursue further acquisitions.

title: 基于千问最新大模型构建，阿里发布全新 AI 店小蜜
content: Alibaba has launched AI Store Assistant (店小蜜), an AI-powered customer service agent built on its latest large‑language model. It handles both pre‑ and post‑sales tasks for e‑commerce merchants. After integration, merchants report a 45% drop in transfers to humans and a 10% boost in conversion compared to fully human support. By March, the system processes nearly 10 million daily conversations for over a million merchants, using Taobao transaction data for domain‑specific fine‑tuning and multimodal enhancements.

title: 2026 05 12 HackerNews
content: Hardware attestation is used by Google and Apple to lock devices into their ecosystems, turning security into a monopoly tool that limits third‑party systems. Local AI is advocated for privacy and latency, but hardware limits and cost still favor the cloud; the mix‑model—cheap cloud for planning, local for execution—may become standard. AI‑generated code can accelerate development, yet it often introduces “god objects” and maintenance debt, requiring human oversight to keep systems modular and maintainable. Security incidents, such as the fictional CVE‑2024‑YIKES supply‑chain attack, expose systemic risks like missing 2FA and automatic merges, urging stronger governance. Obsidian plugin abuse highlights social‑engineering threats, while Gmail’s QR‑code verification increases privacy concerns. Meanwhile, debates over cloud vs. local models, AI maintenance costs, and corporate control of hardware attestations underscore ongoing tensions between efficiency, security, and user freedom.
eventKey: 2026-hackernews
normalizedTitle: 2026 05 12 hackernews
